signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ Hawkish Shift
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkishness) vs RBNZ Governor Breman signaling the cash rate must rise further as inflationary pressures build. With the market bias shifting toward a hike, the crowded spec short faces significant unwind risk. NZD/USD at 0.5894 remains supported by this hawkish pivot despite the 150bp nominal carry deficit vs USD.
signals.watch_level: Watch for a break above 0.5950 to confirm short-squeeze acceleration following the RBNZ meeting.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + CB Divergence
signals.text: NZD/CAD spread at 0.810% identifies a dominant bilateral theme as NZD outperforms (+0.392% avg) while CAD lags (-0.418% avg). The divergence is fundamentally anchored by the RBNZ's hike signal vs the BoC's hold stance. Despite CAD's positive CESI (+8), the hawkish RBNZ shift overrides domestic data beats, driving NZD/CAD higher.
signals.watch_level: Watch for BoC meeting on 10 Jun to determine if CAD can recover on a hawkish hold.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Carry
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7126 supported by a 60bp AONIA-SOFR carry premium and extreme COT LF net long +57,180 (74.5% long). However, IV at 16.7% vs HV30 at 8.2% creates a massive +8.5% VRP, signaling the options market is paying a heavy premium for downside protection. This suggests institutional hedging against a potential long-unwind despite the carry advantage.
signals.watch_level: Watch for VRP compression or a break of 0.7100 to signal the start of a spec long liquidation.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Options Skew + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.57 remains bid on the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.73%). However, 25d RR at -1.34 (Saxo) shows a moderate put skew, indicating the options market is pricing protection against a JPY rally as price tests the 160.00 psychological level. JPY COT LF net short -81,624 confirms a crowded carry trade vulnerable to verbal intervention.
signals.watch_level: 160.00 remains the critical level for BoJ verbal warnings or direct intervention.

signals.title: DXY vs SPX — Correlation Break + Funding Stress
signals.text: DXY vs SPX correlation at -0.641 vs 252d norm of -0.333 (z=+1.81σ) indicates a significant strengthening of the inverse relationship. This suggests the USD is increasingly functioning as a pure risk-off funding proxy; USD bid intensifies on any equity weakness. DXY at 99.35 is supported by this regime shift and a strong positive CESI (+23).
signals.watch_level: Watch for VIX exceeding 20.0 to confirm a transition into a high-volatility USD-bid regime.
